references.title: GetOrganelle: a fast and versatile toolkit for accurate de novo assembly of organelle genomes
references.abstract: GetOrganelle is a fast and versatile toolkit for accurate de novo assembly of organelle genomes. It supports mitochondria, chloroplasts, and other circular genomes from whole-genome sequencing data.
key_findings: Assembles organelle genomes from WGS reads using a seed-and-extend approach
Supports both Illumina and long-read data
Produces complete circular assemblies for most plant species

references.title: NOVOPlasty: de novo assembly of organelle genomes from whole genome sequencing data
references.abstract: NOVOPlasty is a de novo assembler for organelle genomes that extracts organelle reads from WGS data and assembles them into complete circular genomes.
key_findings: Seed-based assembly approach for organelle genomes
Works with both Illumina and Ion Torrent data
Can assemble both mitochondria and chloroplast simultaneously

references.title: MITObim: mitochondrial baiting and iterative mapping
references.abstract: MITObim is a tool for assembling mitochondrial genomes from short reads using a reference-guided baiting and iterative mapping approach.
key_findings: Reference-guided assembly using iterative baiting
Works with distant references
Effective for degraded or low-coverage samples

references.title: PGA: a software suite for rapidly and accurately assembling plant organellar genomes
references.abstract: PGA is a software suite for rapidly and accurately assembling and annotating plant organellar genomes including mitochondria and chloroplasts.
key_findings: Integrated assembly and annotation pipeline
Supports both mitochondria and chloroplast
High accuracy for plant organellar genomes

references.title: CGAS: a comprehensive chloroplast genome analysis suite
references.abstract: CGAS provides a comprehensive pipeline for chloroplast genome analysis including assembly, annotation, comparative genomics, and phylogenetics.
key_findings: End-to-end chloroplast genome analysis pipeline
Includes gene content, structure, codon usage, SSR, SNP analysis
Phylogenetic tree construction integration

references.title: Plant mitochondrial genome evolution and cytoplasmic male sterility
references.abstract: Comprehensive review of plant mitochondrial genome evolution, including repeat-mediated recombination, RNA editing, and cytoplasmic male sterility mechanisms.
key_findings: Plant mitochondrial genomes have unusual features: large size, low gene density, frequent recombination
RNA editing is extensive (C-to-U) and affects phylogenetic analyses
CMS is caused by chimeric mitochondrial genes, restored by nuclear Rf genes

references.title: Genome-wide signatures of plastid-nuclear coevolution point to repeated perturbations of plastid proteostasis systems across angiosperms
references.abstract: ERC analysis reveals genome-wide signatures of plastid-nuclear coevolution, with the strongest signals in proteostasis-related genes.
key_findings: Genome-wide ERC analysis between plastid and nuclear genes
Strongest coevolution signals in proteostasis systems
Repeated perturbations across angiosperms

references.title: OrthoFinder: orthology inference and comparative genomics with scalable orthogroup analysis
references.abstract: OrthoFinder is a fast and accurate tool for orthology inference, providing orthogroups, gene trees, and species tree.
key_findings: Accurate orthogroup inference for comparative genomics
Reconciles gene trees with species tree
Handles gene duplication and loss

references.title: PhenoAssistant: a multi-agent AI system for phenotype analysis
references.abstract: PhenoAssistant demonstrates the manager-agent pattern for AI-driven biological analysis, routing natural language requests to specialized tools.
key_findings: Manager-agent pattern for biological AI systems
Natural language interface to specialized analysis tools
Validated on phenotype analysis tasks

references.title: ERCnet: genome-wide ERC analysis with gene duplication handling
references.abstract: ERCnet provides genome-wide evolutionary rate covariation analysis with proper handling of gene duplication through gene-tree/species-tree reconciliation.
key_findings: Genome-wide ERC analysis with gene duplication support
BXB and R2T branch mapping strategies
Network analysis for coevolution modules

references.title: The multipartite mitochondrial genome of plants: evolutionary dynamics and engineering challenges
references.abstract: Review of plant mitochondrial genome structure, including multi-configuration genomes, repeat-mediated recombination, and implications for genome engineering.
key_findings: Plant mitochondrial genomes exist as multi-configuration molecules
Repeat-mediated recombination creates structural heterogeneity
Implications for genome engineering and synthetic biology

references.title: Cytoplasmic male sterility in plants: mechanisms and applications
references.abstract: Comprehensive review of CMS mechanisms in plants, including chimeric ORFs, restorer-of-fertility genes, and applications in hybrid seed production.
key_findings: CMS caused by chimeric mitochondrial ORFs
Nuclear Rf genes encode PPR proteins that suppress CMS
Widely used in hybrid seed production

references.title: Targeted RNA editing in plant mitochondria
references.abstract: Study of C-to-U RNA editing in plant mitochondria, revealing the mechanisms and specificity of editing factors.
key_findings: C-to-U RNA editing is extensive in plant mitochondria
PPR proteins are the primary editing factors
Editing affects protein function and phylogenetic signals

references.title: IQ-TREE 2: new models and efficient methods for phylogenetic inference
references.abstract: IQ-TREE 2 provides fast and accurate phylogenetic inference with new substitution models and ultrafast bootstrap.
key_findings: Ultrafast bootstrap with UFBoot2
New substitution models for protein and codon data
Scalable to large datasets

references.title: MAFFT multiple sequence alignment software version 7
references.abstract: MAFFT is a widely used multiple sequence alignment program with various alignment strategies for different data types.
key_findings: Multiple alignment strategies (FFT-NS-2, L-INS-i, etc.)
Handles large datasets efficiently
Accurate for diverse sequence types

references.title: trimAl: a tool for automated alignment trimming
references.abstract: trimAl is a tool for the automated removal of poorly aligned regions from multiple sequence alignments.
key_findings: Automated alignment trimming for phylogenetic analysis
Multiple trimming strategies (strict, gappy, etc.)
Improves phylogenetic tree accuracy

references.title: BUSCO: assessing genome assembly and annotation completeness
references.abstract: BUSCO provides measures for quantitative assessment of genome assembly, gene set, and transcriptome completeness based on evolutionarily informed expectations.
key_findings: Universal single-copy orthologs for completeness assessment
Applicable to assemblies, gene sets, and transcriptomes
Standard benchmarking tool for genome projects

references.title: PyHMMER: a Python binding to HMMER
references.abstract: PyHMMER provides Python bindings to HMMER3 for protein domain identification using profile HMMs.
key_findings: Python bindings for HMMER3 sequence analysis
Profile HMM search and annotation
Used in MitoFlow for protein-coding gene prediction

references.title: The evolving landscape of plant mitochondrial genomes
references.abstract: Comprehensive review of plant mitochondrial genome evolution, including size variation, repeat content, recombination dynamics, and gene transfer to nucleus.
key_findings: Plant mitochondrial genomes vary 200-fold in size
Repeat content drives structural dynamism
Gene transfer to nucleus is ongoing

references.title: tRNAscan-SE: improved detection of transfer RNA genes in genomic sequences
references.abstract: tRNAscan-SE identifies tRNA genes in genomic sequences using a combination of tRNA-specific covariance models and heuristics.
key_findings: High sensitivity and specificity for tRNA detection
Covariance model-based search
Standard tool for tRNA annotation

references.title: BLAST+: architecture and applications
references.abstract: BLAST+ is the updated BLAST toolkit providing sequence similarity search capabilities for nucleotide and protein databases.
key_findings: Standard sequence similarity search tool
Supports nucleotide and protein queries
Used in MitoFlow for gene validation and NUMT detection

entities.description: Fast and versatile toolkit for accurate de novo assembly of organelle genomes from WGS data
De novo assembler for organelle genomes using seed-based approach
Reference-guided mitochondrial genome assembly using baiting and iterative mapping
Software suite for rapidly and accurately assembling and annotating plant organellar genomes
Fast and accurate orthology inference for comparative genomics
Genome-wide evolutionary rate covariation analysis with gene duplication handling
Fast and accurate phylogenetic inference with new substitution models
Multiple sequence alignment software with various strategies
Cytoplasmic Male Sterility — maternally inherited inability to produce functional pollen, caused by chimeric mitochondrial genes
Post-transcriptional C-to-U conversion in plant mitochondria, affecting hundreds of sites
Evolutionary Rate Covariation — correlated evolutionary rates between genes indicating functional interaction

properties.application: Detecting coevolution between organelle and nuclear genes

entities.description: NADH dehydrogenase complex of the mitochondrial respiratory chain
Cytochrome c oxidase complex of the mitochondrial respiratory chain
Automated alignment trimming tool for phylogenetic analysis
Assessment of genome assembly and annotation completeness using universal single-copy orthologs
tRNA gene detection in genomic sequences using covariance models
Standard sequence similarity search toolkit for nucleotide and protein databases

properties.application: Gene validation, NUMT detection, homology search
